vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

risk.desc: The fuselage spine may not withstand the nacelle loads without additional reinforcement.
risk.mit: Add rib reinforcement to the fuselage spine.

risk.desc: The avionics bay may overheat if cooling ducts are not properly designed.
risk.mit: Design an efficient cooling duct system for the avionics bay.

risk.desc: The service hatch may not provide sufficient access for maintenance.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the service hatch for better access.

risk.desc: The GPS boom mount may not be securely attached to the fuselage spine.
risk.mit: Add a locking mechanism to the GPS boom mount.

risk.desc: The belly camera window may not be sealed properly against the fuselage.
risk.mit: Ensure the camera window has a proper seal to prevent leaks.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

physics_paths.description: Loads from tilting rotor nacelles are transferred into the fuselage spine via the wing spar socket.
Cooling air flows through the avionics bay to dissipate heat generated by electronics.
Thermal energy from the avionics bay is dissipated into the external environment through heat sinks and cooling ducts.
Motion from tilting rotor nacelles is transferred into the fuselage spine through the wing spar socket.
Service personnel access the avionics bay through a hatch to perform maintenance and upgrades.

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: install wing spar socket before rotor nacelles
align v_tail mount before installing v_tail
align ruddervator mount before installing ruddervator

integration_opportunities.description: Add a service hatch to the avionics bay for easy access to internal components.
Integrate a GPS sensor mount on the front GPS boom for high-altitude mapping.
Install a camera window on the belly for ground observation.

manufacturing_strategy.datum: primary locating faces and rail guides
manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures for FDM parts, toolpaths for CNC machining
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: CMM for dimensional accuracy, visual inspection for surface finish

verification_focus.description: Verify the structural integrity of the fuselage spine under nacelle loads.
Ensure the avionics bay maintains a safe operating temperature.
Check that service access paths are clear and unobstructed.

risk.desc: Thermal cycling from -20 to +50C may cause material fatigue over time.
risk.mit: Conduct accelerated life testing to validate material performance.

risk.desc: Pressure testing for avionics bay may reveal unexpected leaks.
risk.mit: Perform leak detection tests during assembly and validate seals.

risk.desc: Electrical continuity for harnesses may be compromised by improper routing.
risk.mit: Ensure proper routing and strain relief for all electrical harnesses.

risk.desc: Service access for maintenance may be limited by design.
risk.mit: Review design for any potential obstructions and improve access where necessary.

risk.desc: Load testing for nacelle loads may exceed structural limits if not properly designed.
risk.mit: Conduct finite element analysis to validate structural integrity under load.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

physics_paths.description: Rotor thrust loads are transferred through the tilt axis and bearing seats into the wing structure.
The tilt axis allows the nacelle to rotate from 0 to 90 degrees.
Heat generated by the motor is dissipated into the ambient environment.
Electrical power from the ESC is delivered to the motor.
Service personnel can access the motor and ESC for maintenance.

integration_opportunities.description: Integration with wing spar and fuselage spine sockets.
Integration with harness passthrough for motor and ESC connections.
Integration with access ports for field serviceability.

verification_focus.description: Verify load transfer through tilt axis and bearing seats.
Verify electrical connectivity between ESC and motor.
Verify accessibility for motor and ESC maintenance.
